LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF OLYMPIC VIEW REALTY, LLC'S AMENDED MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. ARGUED AND THE COURT TOOK THE MATTER UNDER SUBMISSION. JUDGE: LORETTA M. GIORGI; REPORTER: RHONDA AQUILINA, CSR 	956 -------- AFTER HEARING, THE COURT RULED AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AS TO THE 1ST AND 2ND CAUSES OF ACTION IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF HAS SUCCESSFULLY MET ITS BURDEN. DEFENDANT HAS FAILED TO PROVIDE TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT REGARDING THE DEFINITENESS OF THE CONTRACTUAL TERMS IN QUESTION. THE CLEAR LANGUAGE OF THE CONTRACT DOES NOT SUPPORT DEFENDANT'S INTERPRETATION AND THE EVIDENCE SHOWS DEFENDANT REFUSED TO EXECUTE NEW EASEMENTS AS REQUIRED UNDER THE CONTRACT. MOREOVER, PLAINTIFF NEED NOT WAIT TOO INCUR ACTUAL DAMAGES FROM A BREACH OF A CONTRACT. E.O.C. ORD., INC. V. KAVAKOVICH (1988) 200 CAL.APP. 2D 220, 231. PLAINTIFF IS ENTITLED TO AN AWARD OF ATTORNEY FEES BUT MUST PRESENT THAT BY SEPARATE MOTION SETTING FORTH AMOUNTS TO BE AWARDED. THE COURT SIGNED ORDERS RE: THE PARTIES' OBJECTIONS. PARTIES NOTIFIED OF THE COURT'S RULING BY TELEPHONE. PLAINTIFF TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER.

Plaintiff Olympic View Realty, Llc Amended Motion For Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Aug-25-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF OLYMPIC VIEW REALTY, LLC'S AMENDED MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION.
Appearances: Samorn Selim and Ann M. Heimberger of Manatt Phelps & Phillips, LLP (291.7454) appearing with Barry W. Lee (via Court Call) for plaintiff and moving party; David M. Almaraz of Hamburg Karic Edwards & Martin LLP (310.552.9292) for defendant and responding party;
Argued and the court took the matter under submission. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi; Reporter: Rhonda Aquilina, CSR 956
---
After hearing, the court ruled as follows: Plaintiff's motion for summary adjudication as to the 1st and 2nd causes of action is granted. Plaintiff has successfully met its burden. Defendant has failed to provide triable issues of fact regarding the definiteness of the contractual terms in question. The clear language of the contract does not support defendant's interpretation and the evidence shows defendant refused to execute new easements as required under the contract. Moreover, plaintiff need not wait too incur actual damages from a breach of a contract. E.O.C. Ord., Inc. v. Kavakovich (1988) 200 Cal.App. 2d 220, 231. Plaintiff is entitled to an award of attorney fees but must present that by separate motion setting forth amounts to be awarded. The court signed orders re: the parties' objections. Parties notified of the court's ruling by telephone. Plaintiff to submit a proposed form of order.

AMENDED MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Aug-22-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF OLYMPIC VIEW REALTY, LLC'S AMENDED MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION.
Appearances: Barry W. Lee of Manatt Phelps & Phillips LLP (310.312.4250) for plaintiff and moving party; David M. Almaraz of Hamburg Karic Edwards & Martin LLP (310.552.9292) for defendant and responding party.
The court ruled on the parties' objections as follows: defendant's objections are all overruled except for number 21 which is sustained in part; plaintiff's objections are overruled except for numbers 3, 5, 9 and 10. The matter was discussed and the court continued the matter to August 25, 2011 for argument. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi; Reporter: Angie Diner, CSR 581
